preview_text:
The model rates Belgium as the clear favorite with a 65% win probability, while New Zealand enters with an 11% chance of victory. The underdog thus faces a substantial deficit in the forecast, though not an insurmountable one at the group stage.

The 24% draw probability is the second-most likely outcome, suggesting a competitive match where neither side overwhelms the other. Belgium's advantage manifests as a roughly 41-percentage-point gap between its win and New Zealand's win odds—wide enough to reflect a meaningful quality gap, yet the combined 35% non-Belgium outcome probability indicates the match carries genuine uncertainty and is not a formality.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully. The market prices Belgium's win at 73%, nearly 9 percentage points higher than the model's 65%, while assigning New Zealand only 10% and a draw just 17%. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook direction. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; users should note the gap in confidence around Belgium's likelihood of victory.
